title: zealandia pustulata
intro: Zealandia pustulata, also known as the New Zealand pouhou or pouhou, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to New Zealand. The pouhou is a shrubby plant with clusters of small, yellow daisy-like flowers, and has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties. The pouhou is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including soothing skin irritations and reducing the severity of colds and flu. Pouhou is traditionally prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is also sometimes used in modern herbal preparations, such as tinctures and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
